config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi kepentingan masyarakat secara umum, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Kalau prioritasnya selalu masyarakat di atas individu, mudah sekali muncul pembenaran untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become an excuse for abuse. Individual rights are the guardrails that keep majorities, governments, and institutions from trampling minorities or unpopular people. At the same time, I do not believe rights are absolute in every case, since public safety and basic rules do matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. If we reverse that presumption, we risk treating people as tools for the group instead of as citizens with dignity of their own.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling rentan justru kelompok kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi, bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu demi masyarakat, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak individu sebagai pagar utamanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing makes me nervous because history shows how quickly “society’s interests” can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, or other abuses against unpopular people. I do think there are real cases where individual rights can be limited for public safety or basic order, but those limits should be narrow, necessary, and heavily justified. To me, the default should always be that society serves people, not that people exist mainly to serve society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai pembenar untuk membatasi kebebasan, membungkam suara kecil, atau mengorbankan kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau ketertiban, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk menekan orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari penghormatan pada hak tiap orang, bukan dari kebiasaan mengalahkan individu atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement that society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, that idea has a bad track record because “the public good” has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach against people who had little power. I do think there are narrow cases where rights can be limited for safety or order, but those limits should be exceptional, not the default. A healthy society is not one that crushes individuals for the majority; it is one that protects individual rights precisely so the majority cannot abuse them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
